author: etan_status
content: no, optional isn't an SSZ type. also, fork-choice stores are not an SSZ object... it's a regular python object not an SSZ Container

author: nflaig
content: we have optional in our ssz lib 😄

author: nflaig
content: but I think what potuz meant is encode the fork choice dump as ssz instead of json, which already right now (pre-gloas) is not possible

author: etan_status
content: there is no SSZ schema for the fork choice endpoint, yeah, it's defined as json only

author: nflaig
content: you cna't right now as it returns enums (`validity`) and has this arbitrary `extra_data` field

author: etan_status
content: let's first get unbounded lists to fix the deposits SSZ cap, and to enable nested lists without requiring hundreds of hashes when there is even just a single element in it 🙂 so far only lodestar and nimbus have it.

author: potuz
content: I opened a PR on Prysm for the endpoint. If that format works for you guys let's open it in the API

author: nflaig
content: that works, we can further discuss on the PR

author: jameshe5018
content: https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/pull/608 I think i addressed all open feedback on this so far, how do people feel about this? one thing remains is if we want to just completely remove the get proposer preferences call

author: tersec
content: yeah why is it there?

author: tersec
content: it's not a "pool" in the usual sense

author: tersec
content: and a client not acting as a builder doesn't need it

author: tersec
content: as an externally accessible builder, rather (not self-build)

author: nflaig
content: I can only see it useful for debugging, or if a builder wants to pull proposer preferences from there instead of listening to the sse event

author: jameshe5018
content: i guess then it would be under debug?

author: jameshe5018
content: is that useful enough to keep?

author: tersec
content: why have it at all? `debug` is as hard to turn off as anything

author: tersec
content: because of the checkpoint sync stuff

author: jameshe5018
content: ok i mean i can remove it, i don't have strong feelings for it

author: tersec
content: `debug` isn't real, hasn't been since Jacek force the undoing of the move of getStateV2 back into `debug` some years ago

author: nflaig
content: my default would be no if nobody wants it, we can always add it later, <@1386598056320831509> can you ask builders about this

author: tersec
content: and this is exactly the trap that sets

author: tersec
content: it seems more casual to add to `debug` but it's not

author: jameshe5018
content: i'm leaning towards removing for now and adding if needed in a spearate pr

author: nflaig
content: we can wait for feedback from builders

author: jameshe5018
content: ok i'll just get it ready locally
